point_support.reason: The document states that COVID-19 is mainly transmitted through inhaling air contaminated by droplets and aerosols [doc1:chunk_00024].
The document mentions that infected people exhale virus particles when they breathe, talk, cough, sneeze, or sing [doc1:chunk_00024].
The document indicates that transmission is most likely at close range but can occur over longer distances indoors [doc1:chunk_00024].
The document states that COVID-19 can spread by touching the eyes, nose, or mouth after contact with contaminated surfaces [doc1:chunk_00004].
The document notes that people can spread the virus even if they do not develop symptoms and remain contagious for up to 20 days [doc1:chunk_00004].
External evidence confirms that COVID-19 spreads mainly through the air in tiny droplets when people cough, sneeze, breathe, sing, or talk [node_b:0:81c35db2].
External evidence supports that COVID-19 spreads through droplets from an infected person's mouth or nose [node_c:0:688ca7b0].
External evidence confirms that COVID-19 can spread from someone who is infected but has no symptoms [node_b:4:04360111].
External evidence supports that COVID-19 can spread by touching the mouth, nose, or eyes after contacting contaminated surfaces [node_c:2:5668c8d6].

External evidence confirms that the virus can spread via larger respiratory droplets and smaller aerosols, and people can be contagious without symptoms [node_c:1:da9ca351].

The document states COVID-19 spreads through the air in tiny droplets when people cough, sneeze, breathe, sing, or talk [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00000].
The document mentions droplets can land on the face or be inhaled by others in close proximity or areas with low airflow [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00008].
Close contact, such as sitting next to someone or sharing a car, is noted as increasing the risk of catching COVID-19 [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00011].
Risk factors include living with someone who has COVID-19 and spending time in crowded spaces with poor airflow [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00010].
The document confirms COVID-19 can spread from someone who is infected but has no symptoms [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00007].

The document states that COVID-19 spreads through droplets from an infected person's mouth or nose when they cough, sneeze, speak, sing, or breathe [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00003].

The document states that experts continue to research how COVID-19 spreads and identify high-risk situations [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00005].
External evidence confirms that COVID-19 spreads mainly through inhaling air contaminated by droplets and aerosols from infected people.
